{{Short description="Gauge bosons mediating the weak interaction}}
{{Infobox physics
| name = W and Z bosons
| image = 
| caption = 
| topics = [[Weak interaction]], [[Standard Model]], [[Elementary particle]], [[Boson]], [[Fundamental interaction]]
| composition = [[Elementary particle]]
| statistics = [[Boson]]
| types = W⁺, W⁻, Z⁰
| mass_W = 80.377 ± 0.012 GeV/c²
| mass_Z = 91.1876 ± 0.0021 GeV/c²
| charge = W⁺: +1 e, W⁻: −1 e, Z⁰: 0
| spin = 1
}}

The '''W and Z bosons''' are the [[gauge boson]]s that mediate the [[weak interaction]], one of the four [[fundamental interaction]]s of nature. They were discovered in 1983 at [[CERN]] by the [[UA1 experiment|UA1]] and [[UA2 experiment|UA2]] collaborations, led by [[Carlo Rubbia]] and [[Simon van der Meer]] ([[Nobel Prize in Physics|Nobel Prize]] 1984).

== Types ==
{| class="wikitable"
|-
! Boson !! Symbol !! Charge !! Mass !! Interaction
|-
| W⁺ || W⁺ || +1 e || 80.4 GeV/c² || Charged current
|-
| W⁻ || W⁻ || −1 e || 80.4 GeV/c² || Charged current
|-
| Z⁰ || Z⁰ || 0 || 91.2 GeV/c² || Neutral current
|}

== Role in the weak interaction ==

=== W bosons (charged current) ===
W bosons mediate processes that change [[electric charge]] and [[flavour (particle physics)|flavour]]:
* [[Beta decay]]: <math>d \rightarrow u + W^- \rightarrow u + e^- + \bar{\nu}_e</math>
* [[Muon decay]]: <math>\mu^- \rightarrow \nu_\mu + W^- \rightarrow \nu_\mu + e^- + \bar{\nu}_e</math>

=== Z boson (neutral current) ===
The Z boson mediates interactions where particles do not change charge. First observed at CERN in 1973, this discovery confirmed the [[electroweak theory]].

== Electroweak theory ==
{{Main|Electroweak theory}}
At high energies (~100 GeV), the [[electromagnetic force|electromagnetic]] and [[weak interaction]]s unify into a single [[electroweak force]]. At lower energies, the [[Higgs mechanism]] breaks the symmetry:

* The photon (<math>\gamma</math>) remains massless (infinite range)
* The W⁺, W⁻, and Z⁰ acquire mass (~80–91 GeV/c²), giving the weak force its short range (~10⁻¹⁸ m)

The [[Weinberg angle]] <math>\theta_W</math> relates the masses:
:<math>M_W = M_Z \cos\theta_W</math>

== Properties ==
* Extremely massive — among the heaviest known elementary particles (Z⁰ is ~97× proton mass)
* Short lifetime — ~10⁻²⁵ s (decay rapidly into lighter particles)
* Couple to all [[fermion]]s (quarks and leptons)
* Self-interaction — W and Z bosons interact with each other (predicted by non-abelian gauge theory)

== Discovery ==
The W and Z bosons were discovered at the [[Super Proton Synchrotron]] (SPS) at [[CERN]] in 1983. The discovery was a major triumph of the [[Standard Model]], confirming the [[electroweak theory]].

== See also ==
{{Portal|Physics}}
* [[Boson]]
* [[Electroweak theory]]
* [[Higgs mechanism]]
* [[Standard Model]]
* [[Weak interaction]]

== References ==
{{Reflist|30em}}

[[Category:W and Z bosons| ]]
[[Category:Bosons]]
[[Category:Elementary particles]]
[[Category:Weak interaction]]
